name: Bhimashankar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Moist evergreen forest on the crest of the northern Western Ghats. Trails and temple tracks pass through dense understorey and mixed forest edge, offering good views into both canopy and streamside vegetation.

places_to_stay.name: Forest rest houses (MTDC / Forest Dept)
places_to_stay.description: Basic accommodation inside or near the sanctuary; book in advance.

places_to_stay.name: Small lodges in Bhimashankar village
places_to_stay.description: Simple rooms with access to food and shops.

name: Tamhini Ghat
description: Roadside birding along forested slopes, streams and viewpoints. Classic monsoon ghat section with good visibility into the canopy and frequent mixed flocks along the road edges.

places_to_stay.name: Resorts around Mulshi lake
places_to_stay.description: Convenient base for Tamhini and Mulshi backwater birding.

name: Sinhagad Valley
description: Narrow valley with seasonal streams, dense riparian growth and rocky slopes. A winter hotspot for thrushes and flycatchers, with excellent early-morning activity.

places_to_stay.name: Mulshi / Khadakwasla homestays
places_to_stay.description: Useful if combining multiple nearby sites.

name: Koyna Backwaters & Forests
description: A mosaic of semi-evergreen forest, reservoir edge and hilly villages. Excellent for raptors, hornbills, woodpeckers and forest-edge species. Backwater scanning can be productive in winter.

places_to_stay.name: Koyna MTDC
places_to_stay.description: Reliable and very close to prime birding areas.

places_to_stay.name: Local homestays around Bamnoli / Koynanagar
places_to_stay.description: Good for extended trips and backwater exploration.

name: Kaas Plateau & Forest Fringe
description: Lateritic plateau famous for post-monsoon flowers; edges drop into forested valleys with excellent visibility. Plateau edges hold pipits, larks and raptors; nearby forest has bulbuls, leafbirds and thrushes.

places_to_stay.name: Satara town hotels
places_to_stay.description: Best base for both Kaas and Chalkewadi.

name: Chalkewadi & Thoseghar Valley
location: Windmill plateau above Thoseghar, Satara district
description: High, open plateau with grassland, lateritic rock and deep adjoining valleys. Excellent for harriers, kestrels, pipits and winter migrants. Valley edges can produce vultures and eagles.

places_to_stay.name: Satara hotels
places_to_stay.description: Well-connected and convenient for early starts.

name: Mahabaleshwar Forest Patches
description: Evergreen and semi-evergreen forest pockets around Arthur Seat Road, Venna lake fringes and Old Mahabaleshwar. Good for thrushes, drongos, leafbirds and hornbills.

name: Matheran Plateau & Valleys
description: Vehicle-free lateritic plateau surrounded by deep valleys. Good mix of forest edge, viewpoints and quiet paths offering views of drongos, thrushes, barbets and soaring raptors.

places_to_stay.name: Matheran hill-station hotels
places_to_stay.description: All visits involve walking; choose accommodation close to your preferred entry point.

name: Amboli Ghat
description: Lush, high-rainfall ghat section with superb monsoon birding. Evergreen forest patches along the road support laughingthrushes, sunbirds, flycatchers and endemics.

places_to_stay.name: Amboli hotels and homestays
places_to_stay.description: Plenty of simple to mid-range options close to birding spots.

name: Anshi–Dandeli (Kali Tiger Reserve)
description: Dense evergreen and semi-evergreen forests with exceptional canopy birding. Known for hornbills, woodpeckers, flycatchers and rarities like the Malabar trogon.

places_to_stay.name: Resorts & jungle lodges around Dandeli
places_to_stay.description: Good access to forest drives and guided walks.

name: Agumbe & Someshwara Forest Belt
description: Heavy-rainfall evergreen forest with excellent streamside and canopy birding. Commonly produces Malabar whistling thrush, fairy-bluebirds, woodpeckers and mixed flocks.

places_to_stay.name: Agumbe homestays
places_to_stay.description: Good access to forest trails and viewpoints.

name: Ooty–Avalanche–Emerald Landscape
description: Montane shola–grassland mosaic with distinct high-altitude birdlife. Good for laughingthrushes, wood-pigeons, shola specialists and occasional raptors over the grasslands.

places_to_stay.name: Ooty hotels and homestays
places_to_stay.description: Wide range of stay options with access to multiple shola patches.
